affected_groups: Smaller or less-resourced school maintainers (especially church and foundation)
Teacher unions and professional advocacy bodies
Pupils and families dependent on stable school capacity across maintainer types
content.decision_question: Equity in admissions versus feasibility and fairness of implementation burden across maintainers with unequal administrative capacity
content.evidence_boundary: Contested/unknown: no published costing or capacity budget exists yet for monitoring, exam redesign, or compliance enforcement across state, church, and foundation maintainers, so the scale of the added workload and its distribution across maintainer types cannot currently be verified
content.tension: Implementing SES-proxy criteria and monitoring creates new administrative and workload burdens on schools and teachers that are not currently funded, and may fall disproportionately on smaller or less-resourced maintainers.
content.title: Decision tension A2: S1
value_poles: feasibility and fairness of implementation burden across maintainers with unequal administrative capacity
